observations.recommendation:
1.We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant
send demand letters to those with outstanding past due receivables for more than 7 years;  and 


2. The Municipal Accountant verify, send confirmation letters to NGAs, GOCCs
and LGUs and reconcile any discrepancies. If accounts may be written off, the
procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts as set forth in COA Circular No.
2016-005 shall be observed.

We recommended that management:

1. Prioritize the processing  of   the   titling  of  the   lands  to  prove   claim   of
ownership   and   to   safeguard   government   interest,   and   seek   budgetary
allocations for the pertinent cost thereof; and


2.Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor to reconcile 
the untraceable land properties listed in the books and declaration of real 
property.
observations.reason: There were additional lots identified by the Municipal Treasurer during the physical inventory of lots but the amount recorded in the books was only P11,000,000 out of the P35,000,000 identified.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management direct the Municipal Human Resource
Management Officer to:

1 Review the Leave Cards of employees;


2 Observe the guidelines on mandatory leave under Section 25 of the
above cited regulations;
53

3 Post the leave credits and absences on a monthly basis in order to arrive at accurate balances, and


4 Certify the balances of the leave card while the Municipal Accountant certify the computation of the terminal leave benefits.

It is recommended that management verify and reconcile the accounts for any adjustments and if accounts may be written off, be guided by the procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts as set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
We recommended that Management prepare inventory report for the stockpiles which were still recorded as Other Supplies and Materials Inventory but were already consumed/distributed.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2021, no Inventory Report was submitted by the LDRRM Office to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the GFPS shall monitor the implementation of GAD activities and ensure that the GAD fund will be fully utilized to attain it goals and objectives.
observations.reason: 74.34  % was utilized in CY 2021

observations.recommendation: 1.5 We recommended that management direct the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare, submit and reconcile records with the Municipal Accounting Office monthly to ensure the submission of the financial statements together with other relevant documents promptly pursuant to the stated regulations.

2.6 We recommended that management require:
2.6.1. The Municipal DULWA Administrator to submit monthly billing to the Municipal Treasurer (MT), the MT to prepare report on collected and uncollected water fees including penalties for proper monitoring of the income and receivables accounts and  submit to the Municipal Accountant’s Office; and
2.6.2. Municipal Accountant to draw journal entries to reflect the necessary adjustments to the income and receivable accounts and ensure the preparation of its financial statements using the accrual basis of accounting.

3.7 We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
3.7.1. Require the concerned department heads to coordinate with the Accounting Office and submit  the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) and Physical Count of Inventories for the latter to issue JEV to derecognize the inventories in the books of the LGU; and
3.7.2. Require the periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials. Physical count of unissued inventory items by type which shall be conducted at least once a year as required by GAM and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).

4.9 We recommended that the Management:
4.9.1. Instruct all offices to maintain a separate list or inventory of PPE under their custodianship, update property records, conduct pre-count of these PPE, and submit Departmental RPCPPE to the GSO/Inventory Committee for validation during the annual physical inventory.
4.9.2. Reconstitute the inventory committee composed of adequate number of members or staff to prioritize the completion of the physical inventory of all the LGU’s assets, and prepare the RPCPPE for each type of PPE, reconciled with accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the agency's PPEs; and
4.9.3. Require the GSO/Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to be guided by COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the process of conducting physical count of PPE, preparation of necessary documents, reconciliation procedures, and recording in the books for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

5.7 We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
5.7.1. Municipal Treasurer be directed to prepare the Local Road Network Property Card, Local Road Network Ledger Card, Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and the Report of Local Road Network;
5.7.2. Exercise proper and regular reconciliation between Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and Local Road Network Ledger Card and make the necessary adjustment, where appropriate; and
5.7.3. Municipal Accountant maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical count at year-end and properly depreciate the account and disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

6.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Unit reconcile the discrepancies noted and make the necessary adjustments/corrections, as necessary, in the books to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.

7.5 We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in the reconciliation of accounts of the Disbursing Officer and to coordinate with the previous accounting personnel assigned in the Municipal Accountant’s Office to facilitate reconciliation. On the other hand, management direct the concerned disbursing officer to liquidate his cash advances and/or submit proof on the liquidation made.

8.4 We recommended that the LGU GAD Focal Point System (PGFPS) observe the provision of COA Circular 2014-01 in the preparation and execution of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report.

We recommended that management:
9.6.1 Stop the granting of cash advance to the LCE and other elective officials other than for travelling expenses and confidential fund as prescribed under existing laws; and
9.6.2 The Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the supporting documents before signing the Box “A” of the DV and refrain from processing DVs with incomplete supporting documents. Should there be lacking supporting documents for the claim, the same shall be returned to the claimant.

10.8 We recommended the following:
10.8.1 The LDRRM Officer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile the LDRRMF Investment Plan, Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and RAAO for Continuing Funds, 30% QRF and MOOE; and
10.8.2 The Municipal Accountant/Municipal Budget Officer prepare necessary adjustments for differences noted.

11.7 We recommended that management formulate strategies and intensify the collection efficiency of the waterworks operation of the LGU and strictly implement the provisions embodied in the code of economic enterprises.

.11 We recommended that the Management:
2.11.1 Creates an inventory committee thru an issuance of a written order
and   indicating   therein   the   specific   timeframe   to   complete   the   actual
physical count and deadline of submission of complete physical inventory
report.
2.11.2   Requires   the   inventory   committee   to   prepare   and   submit   the
approved PIP   to   the Office  of the   Auditor   at least ten   (10)   calendar days
before the scheduled start of inventory count.
We recommended that the Management:
1. Creates an inventory committee thru an issuance of a written order
and   indicating   therein   the   specific   timeframe   to   complete   the   actual
physical count and deadline of submission of complete physical inventory
report.

2. Requires   the   inventory   committee   to   prepare   and   submit   the
approved PIP   to   the Office of the   Auditor   at least ten   (10)   calendar days
before the scheduled start of inventory count.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer   record and update all his
Cash Books  for   Cash   in  Local   Treasury,   Cash   in  Bank   and   Cash   Advances
Accounts daily and reconcile its records with the Municipal Accountant’s Office at
least   quarterly   in   order   to   account   any   discrepancies   immediately.   For   Cash
Advances Accounts, the MTO prepare separate cash book for salaries, wages
and allowances and operating expenses per Fund.

We recommended   that   the   Municipal   Treasurer   religiously   follow   the guidelines   in   reporting   dishonored   checks   as   required   under   Section   85   of GAAM on the issuance of Notice of Dishonor and Section 88 on the specific
procedures on the recording and reporting of Dishonored Checks.

We recommend that the BAC see to it that during the Bid opening and evaluation, the criterion “FAILED” should be indicated on the checklist in cases where any item of the requirement is not complied as require in Section 30 of RA No 9184.

We recommended that the BAC ensure that all contracts are made after all
procurement processes have been made.

We recommended that management:
1. Execute contract of lease for all heavy and light equipment before it
shall be released to the Lessee, include e effectivity date and termination of the
lease in the Contract of Lease and require the 50% down payment by the
lessee based on the projected rental;


2. Maintain equipment logbooks showing the time and date of release and
return of the heavy equipment; and

3 Instruct the GSO or In-Charge to keep custody of the equipment keys
and verify details of trip tickets and the amount paid and require the lessee for payments of deficiencies, if any.

We recommended that management observed proper internal control on
receiving, distribution, safekeeping and documentation of welfare relief goods in
order   to   avoid   wastage   and   spoilage   of   goods   and   goods   be   distributed accordingly to intended beneficiaries.
We recommended to conduct HGDG test in evaluating the agency’s PAPs gender sensitivity and responsiveness.

It is recommended that management observe the PCW-NEDA-DILG Joint Circular 2012-01, DILG-PCW-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circulars 2013-01 and 2016-01 and COA Circular 2014-01 in the preparation and execution of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report.

LGU shall refrain from hiring Job Orders/Contracts of services unless it is necessary for the implementation of local projects. Fill up the vacancies instead of hiring JOs. Lastly, maximize the services of available personnel so as not to incur additional expenses.
observations.reason: As of October 2021, only 53% were job orders and vacant positions were filled up.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management observe the PCW-NEDA-DILG Joint Circular 2012-01, DILG-PCW-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circulars 2013-01 and 2016-01 and COA Circular 2014-01 in the preparation and execution of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report.

We recommended that Management:

a. Program the LDRRMF to projects and activities under the four thematic areas, 	 namely, disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and 	 disaster rehabilitation and recovery as provided in Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, refrain from charging activities/expenses under the LDRRFMF that are not disaster related and should be charged to the regular MOOE;

b. Programs, activities, and projects laid down under LDRRMF Investment Plan should be specific; and

c. Prepare inventory report for the stockpiles which were still recorded as Other Supplies and Materials Inventory but were already consumed/distributed.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2020, no Inventory Report was submitted by the LDRRM Office to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that Management;

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to demand the liquidation of cash advances for employees still connected with the agency, otherwise, imposed sanctions by withholding of amount in the salary and other benefits of the employees:

b. Institute appropriate efforts to collect on the unliquidated cash advances, specifically for employees no longer connected with the Municipality by coordinating with the respective mother offices in locating the non-organic personnel with unliquidated cash advances and demand immediate settlement thereof, and

c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to attach a certification that no previous unliquidated cash advances of the employee for their next claimed for cash advances.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the GFPS shall monitor the implementation of GAD activities and ensure that the GAD fund will be fully utilized to attain it goals and objectives.

observations.recommendation: LGU shall refrain from hiring Job Orders/Contracts of services unless it is necessary for the implementation of local projects. Fill up the vacancies instead of hiring JOs. Lastly, maximize the services of available personnel so as not to incur additional expenses.

1.7 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant send demand letters to those with outstanding past due receivables for more than 7 years;  and
1.8. The Municipal Accountant verify, send confirmation letters to NGAs, GOCCs and LGUs and reconcile any discrepancies. If accounts may be written off, the procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts as set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005 shall be observed.

2.11 We recommended that the Management:
2.11.1 Creates an inventory committee thru an issuance of a written order and indicating therein the specific timeframe to complete the actual physical count and deadline of submission of complete physical inventory report.
2.11.2 Requires the inventory committee to prepare and submit the approved PIP to the Office of the Auditor at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory count.

3.9 We recommended that management:
3.9.1 Prioritize the processing of the titling of the lands to prove claim of ownership and to safeguard government interest, and seek budgetary allocations for the pertinent cost thereof; and
3.9.2 Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor to reconcile the untraceable land properties listed in the books and declaration of real property.

4.12 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer  record and update all his Cash Books for Cash in Local Treasury, Cash in Bank and Cash Advances Accounts daily and reconcile its records with the Municipal Accountant’s Office at least quarterly in order to account any discrepancies immediately. For Cash Advances Accounts, the MTO prepare separate cash book for salaries, wages and allowances and operating expenses per Fund.

5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer religiously follow the guidelines in reporting dishonored checks as required under Section 85 of GAAM on the issuance of Notice of Dishonor and Section 88 on the specific procedures on the recording and reporting of Dishonored Checks.

6.6 We recommended that the BAC see to it that during the bid opening and evaluation, the criterion “Failed” should be indicated on the checklist in cases where any item of the requirement is not complied as required in Section 30 of RA No. 9184.
7.5 We recommended that the BAC ensure that all contracts are made after all procurement processes have been made.

8.7 We recommended that management:
8.7.1 Execute contract of lease for all heavy and light equipment before it shall be released to the Lessee, include effectivity date and termination of the lease in the Contract of Lease and require the 50% down payment by the lessee based on the projected rental;
8.7.2 Maintain equipment logbooks showing the time and date of release and return of the heavy equipment; and
8.7.3 Instruct the GSO or In-Charge to keep custody of the equipment keys and verify details of trip tickets and the amount paid and require the lessee for payments of deficiencies, if any.

10.11 We recommended that management observed proper internal control on receiving, distribution, safekeeping and documentation of welfare relief goods in order to avoid wastage and spoilage of goods and goods be distributed accordingly to intended beneficiaries.

It is recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to retrieve the documents on sale of unserviceable properties including the IIRUP and submit to the Municipal Accountant as basis for adjustments of the accounts.

We recommended that management:


a.  Undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the payable accounts. If the analysis/review of the accounts/funds is not possible due to absence of records and documents, the agency head to request for write-off and/or adjustment of account balances from COA, supported by list of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts pursuant to COA Circular 97-001; 

b. Effect the necessary adjustment to revert to the unappropriated fund the balance of prior years’ payables which are not supported with valid claims; and

c. Record only payables with DVs and/or Payroll with supporting documents.

We recommended that Management:

a. Ensure that adequate internal controls are placed in the management of cash, cash items and unused accountable forms, e.g. safes for cash, storerooms for supplies/accountable forms and unissued equipment, lock and key for offices, etc.

b. Install cubicles or other appropriate enclosures to secure and protect cash and accountable forms against risks of loss due to fortuitous events.

c. Municipal Treasurer to establish an effective internal control system in the monitoring of official receipts issued to collectors and the Municipal Treasurer to notify the Municipal Accountant on any unliquidated collections so that the amount will be recognized immediately as income by the agency and receivable from the accountable officer in cases where there is no cash presented together with the official receipts.

It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require all the accountable officers to remit their collections daily and intact to the liquidating officer and prepare their RCDs at the end of business day upon remittance of collections. Ensure that the collectors adhere to the existing rules and regulations on the receipt and remittance/deposit of all collections handled by them to prevent loss and/or misuse of government funds.  Further, for the Municipal Treasurer to regularly monitor all collectors so that collections of the Municipality are correctly receipted, accounted and recorded in the books and properly reported in the period in which they were collected.

It is recommended that management direct the person responsible for the preparation and issuance of the Purchase Orders to see to it that these are completely filled up including the agreed specifications, terms and conditions.

It is recommended that the Municipal Human Resource Management Office observe the required number of days to be retained after monetization and require the submission of justification for the monetization of 50% or more of their accumulated leave credits and/or in excess of the maximum of 30 days monetization in compliance  Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order (EO) No. 292.

We recommended the following:

a. Cause the attendance to seminars and trainings related to GAD of officials and employees whose functions are GAD related to update their knowledge and build their competencies in the execution of GAD related programs and activities; and

b. Conduct HGDG test in evaluating the agency’s PAPs gender sensitivity and responsiveness.
observations.reason: Not implemented. HGDG test was only done on the GAD Plan PAPs.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
It is recommended that management closely monitor the implementation of the project at the site to ensure good harvest of seaweeds especially that the implementation of the project was delayed.

It is recommended that management shall refrain from procuring using the mode of reimbursements and strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR in using the appropriate mode of procurement.

It is recommended that the person responsible for the preparation and issuance of the Purchase Orders be directed to see to it that the date of acceptance by the supplier/contractor is indicated in the purchase order so that the stipulation on liquidated damages for late delivery can be validly imposed.

It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the undeposited collections as soon as possible and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1 by depositing the collections as well as the collections turned over to him with the AGDB daily or not later than the next banking day

It is recommended that Management:    

1. Ensure that PPAs funded out of the 20% DF are fully implemented within the period specified in the AIP.  For PPAs that are still unimplemented and on-going, positive efforts be made to fast track the completion of the PPAs; and

2. Ensure close monitoring of the implementation of the PPAs identified in AIP.

It is recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their balances of the Cash in Local Treasury at least quarterly as required in the above-regulation and to discuss the discrepancies for reconciliation and adjustments to each record.

It is recommended that Management;

a.Direct the Municipal Accountant to demand the liquidation of cash advances for employees still connected with the agency, otherwise, imposed sanctions by withholding of amount in the salary and other benefits of the employees:

b.Institute appropriate efforts to collect on the unliquidated cash advances, specifically for employees no longer connected with the Municipality by coordinating with the respective mother offices in locating the non-organic personnel with unliquidated cash advances and demand immediate settlement thereof, and

c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to attach a certification that no previous unliquidated cash advances of the employee for their next claimed for cash advances.

It is recommended that management shall refrain from procuring using the mode of reimbursements and strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR in using the appropriate mode of procurement.

It is recommended that the person responsible for the preparation and issuance of the Purchase Orders be directed to see to it that the date of acceptance by the supplier/contractor is indicated in the purchase order so that the stipulation on liquidated damages for late delivery can be validly imposed.

It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the undeposited collections as soon as possible and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 69 of the PD No. 1445 and Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1 by depositing the collections as well as the collections turned over to him with the AGDB daily or not later than the next banking day

It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer update his cashbook  record,  balance it daily,  record the transactions in writing to the pages provided in the cashbooks and reconcile  accountabilities with accounting records at least once in a quarter.
It is recommended that the GFPS shall monitor the implementation of GAD activities and ensure that the GAD fund will be fully utilized to attain it goals and objectives.

LGU shall refrain from hiring Job Orders/Contracts of services unless it is necessary for the implementation of local projects. Fill up the vacancies instead of hiring JOs. Lastly, maximize the services of available personnel so as not to incur additional expenses.

Recommendations:
1.6 We recommended that management verify and reconcile the accounts for any adjustments and if accounts may be written off, be guided by the procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts as set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Recommendations:
2.6 We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to retrieve the documents on sale of unserviceable properties including the IIRUP and submit to the Municipal Accountant as basis for adjustments of the accounts.

Recommendations:
3.7 We recommended that management:
a.  Undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the payable accounts. If the analysis/review of the accounts/funds is not possible due to absence of records and documents, the agency head to request for write-off and/or adjustment of account balances from COA, supported by list of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts pursuant to COA Circular 97-001;
b. Effect the necessary adjustment to revert to the unappropriated fund the balance of prior years’ payables which are not supported with valid claims; and
c. Record only payables with DVs and/or Payroll with supporting documents.

Recommendations:
4.4 We recommended that Management:
a. Ensure that adequate internal controls are placed in the management of cash, cash items and unused accountable forms, e.g. safes for cash, storerooms for supplies/accountable forms and unissued equipment, lock and key for offices, etc.
b. Install cubicles or other appropriate enclosures to secure and protect cash and accountable forms against risks of loss due to fortuitous events.
c. Municipal Treasurer to establish an effective internal control system in the monitoring of official receipts issued to collectors and the Municipal Treasurer to notify the Municipal Accountant on any unliquidated collections so that the amount will be recognized immediately as income by the agency and receivable from the accountable officer in cases where there is no cash presented together with the official receipts.

Recommendation:
5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require all the accountable officers to remit their collections daily and intact to the liquidating officer and prepare their RCDs at the end of business day upon remittance of collections. Ensure that the collectors adhere to the existing rules and regulations on the receipt and remittance/deposit of all collections handled by them to prevent loss and/or misuse of government funds.
5.8 Further, for the Municipal Treasurer to regularly monitor all collectors so that collections of the Municipality are correctly receipted, accounted and recorded in the books and properly reported in the period in which they were collected.

Recommendation:
6.5 We recommended that management direct the person responsible for the preparation and issuance of the Purchase Orders to see to it that these are completely filled up including the agreed specifications, terms and conditions.

Recommendation:
8.5 We recommended that the Municipal Human Resource Management Office observe the required number of days to be retained after monetization and require the submission of justification for the monetization of 50% or more of their accumulated leave credits and/or in excess of the maximum of 30 days monetization in compliance  Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order (EO) No. 292.

Recommendations:
11.5 We recommended the following:
a. Cause the attendance to seminars and trainings related to GAD of officials and employees whose functions are GAD related to update their knowledge and build their competencies in the execution of GAD related programs and activities; and
b. Conduct HGDG test in evaluating the agency’s PAPs gender sensitivity and responsiveness.

Recommendation:
12.3 We recommended that management observe the PCW-NEDA-DILG Joint Circular 2012-01, DILG-PCW-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circulars 2013-01 and 2016-01 and COA Circular 2014-01 in the preparation and execution of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report.

Recommendations:
13.17 We recommended that Management:
a. Program the LDRRMF to projects and activities under the four thematic areas, 	namely, disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and 	disaster rehabilitation and recovery as provided in Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG 	JMC No. 2013-1, refrain from charging activities/expenses under the LDRRFMF that are not disaster related and should be charged to the regular MOOE;
b. Programs, activities, and projects laid down under LDRRMF Investment Plan should be specific; and
c. Prepare inventory report for the stockpiles which were still recorded as Other Supplies and Materials Inventory but were already consumed/distributed.

Recommendation:
14.7 We recommended that management submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Recommendation:
15.9 We recommended that management closely monitor the implementation of the project at the site to ensure good harvest of seaweeds especially that the implementation of the project was delayed.

It is recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to make cut-off time for the collections and deposit the collections intact and in full for all funds of the Municipality. It is also recommended that the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference noted and make the necessary adjustments, if any.

It is recommended that management direct the Property Officer to prepare and submit inventory of insurable physical assets, determine appraisal value and insure physical assets with the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) and include the premium in the Annual Budget of the agency or allocate portion of the premium to the LDRR Funds as mandated by the above cited regulations.

It is recommended that management direct all the Department Heads to submit the claims of their offices during the year of its occurrence /delivery to the Accounting Office for the proper recording of transactions as provided by the law.
It is recommended that management should pass resolution authorizing the grant of and specifying the rates of additional allowances to these officials.

It is recommended that Management:

1. Ensure that PPAs funded out of the 20% DF are fully implemented within the period specified in the AIP.  For PPAs that are still unimplemented and on-going, positive efforts be made to fast track the completion of the PPAs; and

2. Ensure close monitoring of the implementation of the PPAs identified in AIP.

It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the undeposited collections as soon as possible and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1 by depositing the collections as well as the collections turned over to him with the AGDB daily or not later than the next banking day
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation of the balances. As of December 31, 2018, the Cash in Local Treasury Account is P6,262,854.12

The inventory of PPE was not completed per validation of the reports submitted. Some items found in the Actual Inventory were not identified per books. Unserviceable Properties which were already disposed on prior years were not adjusted in the books and no report were submitted to the Accountant for adjustments.

No inventory made for Motor Vehicle and Heavy Equipment

This is a reiteration for CY 2018 Findings.

It is recommended that Management:

1. Ensure that PPAs funded out of the 20% DF are fully implemented within the period specified in the AIP.  For PPAs that are still unimplemented and on-going, positive efforts be made to fast track the completion of the PPAs; and

2. Ensure close monitoring of the implementation of the PPAs identified in AIP.

observations.recommendation: MDC and the MPDC as the MDC secretariat shall ensure that PAPs funded out of the 20% EDF are implemented within the period specified in the AIP. observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to conduct an immediate review of his records for the Cash in Vault accounts of the General, Special Education and Trust Funds and reconcile the difference between the cashbooks and ledgers by coordinating with the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2018

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to make cut-off time for the collections and deposit the collections intact and in full for all funds of the Municipality. It is also recommended that the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference noted and make the necessary adjustments, if any.

The Cash – Local Treasury per books (ledgers) in the amount of P3,981,100.04 differ with the Cash Book as of December 31, 2018 amounting to P6,261,854.12, due to the failure of the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records which is not in accordance with Section 181(c), Volume I of the  Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM), hence, the Cash- Local Treasury account could not be relied upon.

Section 181 (c ), Volume 1 of GAAM states that:
The Accountable Officer shall reconcile the book balance with the cash on hand daily. He shall foot and close at the end of each month. The Accountable Officer and the Accountant shall reconcile their books of accounts at least quarterly.

The Cash- Local Treasury account in the Financial Statements/General Ledger as compared to the Cash Book for all funds is shown as follows:
The above table shows that the Cash in Local Treasury presented in the Financial Statement is greater by P2,280,754.08 than the records of the Treasurer’s Cash Book. Interview with the Municipal Treasurer revealed that there were no reconciliation made with the Municipal Accountant since the former’s assumption of office in August 2009. Hence, resulted to accumulations of unreconciled balances as of December 31, 2018.

The audit team has made an on-going reconciliation of accounts to determine the accuracy of balances in the Cash in Local Treasury and to establish the correct accountability of the Municipal Treasurer in the Cash Examination Report.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their balances of the Cash in Local Treasury at least quarterly as required in the above-regulation and to discuss the discrepancies for reconciliation and adjustments to each record.

It is recommended that Management;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to demand the liquidation of cash advances for employees still connected with the agency, otherwise, imposed sanctions by withholding of amount in the salary and other benefits of the employees:
Institute appropriate efforts to collect on the unliquidated cash advances, specifically for employees no longer connected with the Municipality by coordinating with the respective mother offices in locating the non-organic personnel with unliquidated cash advances and demand immediate settlement thereof, and
c.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to attach a certification that no previous unliquidated cash advances of the employee for their next claimed for cash advances.

It is recommended that management direct the Property Officer to prepare and submit inventory of insurable physical assets, determine appraisal value and insure physical assets with the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) and include the premium in the Annual Budget of the agency or allocate portion of the premium to the LDRR Funds as mandated by the above cited regulations.

It is recommended that management shall refrain from procuring using the mode of reimbursements and strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR in using the appropriate mode of procurement.

It is recommended that the person responsible for the preparation and issuance of the Purchase Orders be directed to see to it that the date of acceptance by the supplier/contractor is indicated in the purchase order so that the stipulation on liquidated damages for late delivery can be validly imposed.

It is recommended that management direct all the Department Heads to submit the claims of their offices during the year of its occurrence /delivery to the Accounting Office for the proper recording of transactions as provided by the law.
It is recommended that management should pass resolution authorizing the grant of and specifying the rates of additional allowances to these officials.

It is recommended that the Management, through the SWMB: (a) consider developing concrete programs and projects that are supportive of proper solid waste management and reduction of waste at source e.g. reuse, reduce and recycle of non-biodegradable materials; (b) conduct information and education campaigns on the hazards of open burning and strictly monitor compliance thereof especially at the barangay level; and (c) strengthen the information campaign by putting up signages in public places displaying the acts prohibited under Section 48 of R.A NO. 9003 and the corresponding sanctions thereof.
It is also recommended the integration of SWM orientation upon renewal/application of mayor’s permit and business licenses as part of their strategies to ensure the viability and effective implementation of solid waste management program in the entire municipality.

It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer update his cashbook  record,  balance it daily,  record the transactions in writing to the pages provided in the cashbooks and reconcile  accountabilities with accounting records at least once in a quarter.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that in the succeeding fiscal years, management shall include sufficient appropriation in the Annual Budget for Senior Citizens and Persons with Disabilities and for Implementation of the programs of the Local Councils for the protection of children as required under RA No. 9344, RA No. 9442 amending RA No. 7277 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2014-59.

It is also recommended that prepare a comprehensive plan and budget specifically for SC and PWD and implementation of the programs of the LCPC.  The same shall be approved by the Sangguniang Bayan and incorporated in the Annual Budget.

It is recommended that Management appropriates 5% in the General Fund Annual Budget of the LGU for LDRRMF and any adjustment made therein to be allocated properly to  30% QRF and 70% Mitigation Fund as required under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-01.

It is also recommended that for CY 2019, Management shall also allocate on top of the current 30% QRF the P120,000.00 difference, shall be included in the LDRRMFIP duly and the same shall be transferred to the special trust fund.

It is recommended that Management revert back the CY2011-2012 unexpended LDRRMF to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund to be available for other social services upon enactment by the Local Sanggunian.

It is recommended that in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP, it shall present the 30% allocation for QRF in lump-sum and differentiate the fund source of the PPAs, be it in the 70% current appropriation or continuing and provide a separate caption for PPAs to be charged to the previous year’s unexpended fund; as shown below:
It is recommended that the GFPS shall monitor the implementation of GAD activities and ensure that the GAD fund will be fully utilized to attain it goals and objectives.
observations.reason: Management direct the offices concerned to implement the activities and programs indicated in the GAD plan.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant and personnel tasked with the preparation of the payroll to see to it that monthly deductions are made on the payrolls of employees who availed of the Short-Term Loan Program of the Pag-ibig Fund.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall return the transferred fund from the special trust fund to the general fund and make the necessary adjusting journal entry to correct the error made.

It is recommended that if unexpended balances of the LDRRMF will be utilized for new expenditure items, it will be included in the LDRRMFIP.  It will then be evaluated by the LDRRMC.  The latter will then recommend its inclusion in the AIP and the same shall be approved by the local sanggunian and LCE.  If the AIP was already approved, the AIP should be revised and the same shall be approved by the local sanggunian and LCE.

It is also recommended that the MDRRMO completely fill up the LDRRMFIP form specifically the funding source column.

It is recommended that Management:

Ensure that PAPs funded out of the 20% EDF are implemented within the period specified in the AIP. For PAPs that are still unimplemented and on-going, positive efforts be made to fast track the completion of the PAPs; and

Ensure close monitoring of the implementation of the PAPs identified in AIP.
It is recommended that the BuB Focal Person shall ensure the implementation and completion of BuB PAPs and the utilization of the allocated budget and appropriated funds.

Management shall observe strict Compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.  Submit the requested documents to facilitate inspection by the RTITS. Demand from the contractor the necessary repair of the water system at Barangay Sebucao for it to be operational and be utilized by the intended beneficiaries.
GAD Focal Point System shall execute full implementation of the GAD activities identified in the GAD Plan.

1.Management should exert extra effort to cause the liquidation and settlement of all outstanding cash advances pursuant to COA Cir. No. 97-002.  Henceforth, require refund of all outstanding cash advances which are no longer needed or has not been used for two (2) months or more.  
     
2.Refrain from granting another cash advance to officers and employees with previous unsettled unliquidated cash advances.

3.Enforce sanctions as prescribed in the circular for non-compliance thereof.

4.Withhold the salaries and other emoluments of the concerned officials and employees for failure to render liquidation and settlement of long outstanding cash advances pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 19, 1997.

observations.reason: Launching of the programs was late.

observations.recommendation: Management shall ensure that fund transfers from various barangays as counterparts and to implementing agency are supported with complete documentation pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and the Municipal Accountant properly records the transfer of fund to implementing agencies as receivable for proper accounting and to determine accountability of the transferred funds.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: The Sangguniang Bayan, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer shall ensure that in attesting and enacting a supplemental budget, it is supported with funds actually available which are funds actually in possession or in custody of the Municipal Treasurer, to avoid any legal effects arising on the noncompliance with Section 321 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Management shall ensure that government funds are effectively, efficiently and economically managed and utilized and hire qualified operators for continuous operation of the serviceable heavy equipment.
observations.managementAction: Heavy equipment with useful life were repaired/rehabilitated.

observations.reason: For the year 2016, the LGU still pays its suppliers out of reimbursements to facilitate convenience and efficiency.

observations.recommendation:
1.Refrain from appropriating PAPs that are not in accordance with the Joint Memorandum Circular No 2011-1 and include only those that are related or connected to the implementation of development projects; and

2.Avoid utilizing the 20% Development Fund for expenditures not related to and/or connected to the implementation of development projects, activities and programs.
observations.reason: Municipal Plaza is still one of the PPAs funded from 20% EDF.

observations.recommendation:
1.Refrain from charging expenses which are not prioritized under the Section 100 and 272 of RA 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars No. 1, s. 1998, April 14, 1998; and

2.Exercise prudence in the utilization of Special Education Fund and ensure that SEF will be utilized only to those that will fulfill the objectives and purposes to which SEF were established.
observations.reason: The LGU still incurs expenses which are not priority expenditures in accordance with RA No. 7160.

observations.recommendation:
1.Require the  Accountable Officers concerned to  immediately restitute  the missing funds so that it can be properly accounted  or applied to taxpayers accounts.

2.Institute administrative and/or  legal sanctions against them as provided under Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code (RPC) for failure to render accounts as amended by R.A. 1060  which states that:

“The failure of  a public officer to have duly forthcoming any public funds or  property with which he is chargeable, upon demand by any duly authorized officer shall be prima facie evidence that he has put the missing funds or property to personal use”(Underscoring ours)

3.Further, the Municipal Treasurer should be directed to closely monitor the accountabilities of the collectors.

4.Direct the liquidating officer to thoroughly examine  every section of the RCD to ensure that all Accountable Forms are duly accounted for.

5.Ensure that all collections of  the day are remitted to the Municipal Treasurer as provided under existing laws and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The regularly employed AOs who have unliquidated balances were forced to monetize their leave credits to partially settle their obligations and the balance were deducted from their salaries.

Demand letters have been issued to concerned AOs.

The AOs who still have outstanding balances were no longer issued AFs.

The Municipal Treasurer had purchased cash box for each collector to ensure remittance of collections at the end of the day.

observations.recommendation:
1.Management should exert extra effort to cause the liquidation and settlement of all outstanding cash advances pursuant to COA Cir. No. 97-002.  Henceforth, require refund of all outstanding cash advances which are no longer needed or has not been used for two (2) months or more.  
     
2.Refrain from granting another cash advance to officers and employees with previous unsettled unliquidated cash advances.

3.Enforce sanctions as prescribed in the circular for non-compliance thereof.

4.Withhold the salaries and other emoluments of the concerned officials and employees for failure to render liquidation and settlement of long outstanding cash advances pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 19, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is not approving the grant of cash advances for officers and employees with outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
1.Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the designated Supply Officer to submit to the Accounting Office, Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued, supported with the Requisition and Issue Slip so that the Inventory Account could be adjusted in the books.

2.Require the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant and the designated Supply Officer to maintain Stock Cards and Property Ledger Cards for Supplies, Property Plant and Equipment in their custody to account the receipts and disposition of the same. The balance per Stock Cards, should always reconcile with the Ledger Cards maintained in the accounting office.
observations.managementAction: The designated supply officer has started to maintain stock cards for supplies and materials and mindful of issuing RIS while the Municipal Accountant has started to do the same for property ledger cards for supplies and PPE.
observations.reason: Stock Cards and Property Cards are not updated.

To date, no disposal of heavy and light  equipment were made.  Some are still littered within the premises of the municipal building. Nonetheless, these  equipment will be transferred upon completion of the construction of the new motor pool.

observations.recommendation:
1.Direct the personnel in charge in the Motorpool to strictly follow the existing policy on the renting or leasing of Heavy and Light Equipment.  


2.The equipment should only be released from the motor pool/depot upon presentation of the Official Receipt issued by the Municipal Treasurer, evidencing payment of rental by the lessee.

3.There is a need to re-visit the existing ordinance regarding Equipment Rental for possible enhancement or revision.
observations.reason: A revised revenue code had been in effect.

observations.recommendation:
1.There is a need to re-visit the 10-year  Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan and implement the same  in compliance with RA 9003 and the LGU Ordinance adopting the same.

2.Convene the Solid Waste Management Board and the Technical Working Group  for this purpose.

3.Provide appropriate funding for SWM Programs to include re-orientation and continued advocacy on Environmental Sanitation and Solid Waste Management Programs in conformance with Republic Act 9003.

4. Assign cleaners in the different market areas and provide sufficient water supply to maintain sanitation and cleanliness.

 5.Direct the Market Supervisor to require garbage and waste generators in the market premises to properly manage their waste to eliminate eye sores and foul smells in this densely populated place.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation

observations.recommendation:
1.Management thru the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator, together with the Municipal Development Council,  carefully evaluate every proposed project indicated in the Project Profile of the proponent offices and ensure to include only those outlined under the DBM-DILG Joint Cir. No. 2011-001.  Ensure that only those most meaningful and rooted developmental projects and programs will be considered for consolidation in the Annual Investment Plan.
	
2. The Budget Officer should refrain from incurring obligation to these funds’ expenditures that are not aligned to the guidelines above mentioned.

3.The provisions provided for in Joint DILG – Budget Joint Memorandum Circular     2011-001 dated April 13, 2011 otherwise,  the  officers concerned will be facing sanctions as provided in Sec. 5.0 of the same circular.
observations.reason: Still include Municipal Park it the 20% EDF appropriation.
